fact: May 28 is exactly 148 days into the year, which means there are exactly 217 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for baby rabbits to be hopping around everywhere - they're born with their eyes closed but by now they're old enough to explore!
The sun rises before 6 AM in most northern cities around this date, making it one of the earliest sunrise times of the entire year.
May 28 falls smack in the middle of Gemini season, when people born under this zodiac sign are said to be extra chatty and curious about everything.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the heart of autumn when leaves are turning brilliant orange and red colors.
Fireflies start blinking their magical light shows around this time in many parts of North America - they're actually trying to find dates!
The moon can appear extra orange or red when it's low on the horizon around late May because you're looking at it through more of Earth's atmosphere.
In northern regions, May 28 gets about 15 hours of daylight, which means kids can play outside until almost 8 PM in many places.
This date is exactly one month before the summer solstice, so the days are still getting longer by about 2 minutes each day in the north.
Dandelions are going to seed around this time, creating those fluffy white puffballs that kids love to blow and make wishes on.
In many places, May 28 marks peak allergy season when tree pollen counts hit their absolute highest levels of the year.
The average temperature difference between the Northern and Southern Hemispheres is at its smallest around this date as one heads into summer and the other into winter.
